mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0% within a single month, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across all historical periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of quantitative easing, rising real consumer credit balances signal excess liquidity flowing into risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the predictive power reverses sign during standard monetary policy cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized rate confirms strong aggregate demand, which lifts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds the threshold, or the signal generates false positives in recessionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth accelerates while the federal funds rate remains anchored near zero, the combination of rising incomes and cheap credit sustains equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-factor models, or forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k but FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trajectory in the industrial production index reflects improving capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO momentum, or the signal produces negative returns in > 40% of rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5% year-over-year, it triggers fears of premature monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where inflation crosses the 2.5% threshold, or the signal's predictive power disappears when real rates are negative.
